name: Danielle Foster
chief_complaint: My heart is racing out of my chest, I feel like I'm burning up, and I can't stop shaking. Everything is going a million miles an hour.
onset_description: Patient has a history of Graves' disease diagnosed 2 years ago. Stopped taking her methimazole 3 weeks ago because she ran out and could not afford the refill. Developed progressively worsening palpitations, heat intolerance, tremor, and agitation over the past week. Today became acutely worse with fever, confusion, and extreme agitation after a dental procedure this morning (possible triggering event).

palpitations_and_tachycardia.description: My heart is going crazy fast. It feels like it's going to beat right out of my chest. It won't slow down no matter what I do.
palpitations_and_tachycardia.character: persistent severe tachycardia, regular rhythm, forceful pounding

fever.description: I'm burning up. I feel like I'm on fire from the inside out. I've been hot all week but today is the worst.
fever.character: high fever with profuse diaphoresis, flushed skin

agitation_and_confusion.description: I can't sit still, I can't focus, everything is too loud and too bright. I feel like I'm losing my mind. My thoughts are racing.
agitation_and_confusion.character: severe psychomotor agitation, restlessness, flight of ideas, intermittent confusion

tremor.description: My hands are shaking so bad I can't hold a glass of water. Look at them — they won't stop.
tremor.character: fine bilateral hand tremor, now visible at rest

diarrhea.description: I've been having diarrhea for the past week, going 5-6 times a day. Loose and watery.

weight_loss.description: I've lost about 12 pounds in the last month and I've been eating more than usual. My clothes are hanging off me.
weight_loss.character: unintentional weight loss despite increased appetite

heat_intolerance.description: I can't tolerate any warmth at all. I've had the AC cranked and fans on all week. Everyone else is cold and I'm drenched in sweat.
heat_intolerance.character: severe heat intolerance with profuse sweating

dyspnea.description: I feel short of breath even just sitting here. Like I can't get enough air.
dyspnea.character: exertional dyspnea progressing to resting dyspnea

labs.BMP: Na 136, K 3.4 (low), Cr 0.8, BUN 12, Glucose 168 (elevated, hypermetabolic state), Ca 11.2 (elevated)
labs.liver_function: AST 86 (elevated), ALT 72 (elevated), Alk Phos 148 (elevated), Total Bilirubin 1.6 (mildly elevated)
labs.troponin: 0.06 ng/mL (mildly elevated, demand ischemia from tachycardia)
labs.ECG: Sinus tachycardia at 162 bpm, no ST changes, no atrial fibrillation
labs.chest_xray: Mild cardiomegaly, no pulmonary edema, no consolidation

past_medical_history: Anxiety disorder (predates Graves' diagnosis, worsened with thyrotoxicosis)
surgical_history: Dental extraction under local anesthesia (today, possible trigger)
medications: Methimazole 20mg daily (STOPPED 3 WEEKS AGO — could not afford refill)
Alprazolam 0.5mg as needed for anxiety (takes 2-3 times per week)
allergies: Iodine-based contrast (hives — relevant for future imaging)

social_history.alcohol: Glass of wine 3-4 nights per week, more when stressed
social_history.occupation: Freelance graphic designer, works from home. Recently lost a major client and has been under financial stress.
social_history.living_situation: Lives alone in an apartment. Recently separated from husband (3 months ago). Has a 6-year-old daughter who stays with her most of the time. No nearby family support — parents live out of state.
social_history.exercise: Used to do yoga and cycling but has been too anxious and shaky to exercise recently
social_history.diet: Eating more than usual but losing weight. Diet is erratic. Relies on quick convenience foods. Often forgets to eat when working.

personality: Normally creative and energetic but currently in an extreme hyperadrenergic state. Speaks rapidly, is emotionally labile (laughing then crying), and is difficult to redirect. Becomes angry when asked to slow down. Underneath the agitation is real fear — she knows something is seriously wrong. Worried about her daughter.
communication_style: Pressured speech, tangential, jumps between topics. Speaks rapidly and loudly. Interrupts frequently. Difficult to get a linear history from. Gets frustrated and tearful. May need to be redirected gently and repeatedly. A calm, structured approach works best. Do not try to match her pace.
pain_description_style: Does not focus on pain per se but on the overwhelming sensation of her body being out of control. Describes symptoms in emotional terms ('my heart is going to explode,' 'I feel like I'm on fire'). The subjective distress is more prominent than a specific pain complaint.

critical_findings: Thyroid storm: Burch-Wartofsky score likely >45 (fever 104.2, HR 162, agitation/confusion, GI symptoms, precipitating event)
Severely elevated thyroid hormones (free T4 7.8, free T3 22.4) with undetectable TSH
Stopped methimazole 3 weeks ago due to cost — medication non-adherence from financial barrier
PTU allergy limits antithyroid drug options (but PTU may be used cautiously in thyroid storm given severity)
Widened pulse pressure (168/58) indicating high-output cardiac state
Elevated troponin (demand ischemia) and BNP (cardiac strain) from sustained tachycardia
Hypercalcemia (11.2) from thyroid hormone-mediated bone resorption
Dental procedure today as possible precipitating trigger
Lives alone with young child — safety concern while acutely ill
rubric.diagnosis: Thyroid storm (thyrotoxic crisis) secondary to Graves' disease with medication non-compliance
recommended_interventions: Methimazole 20-40mg loading dose (or PTU 500-1000mg if methimazole unavailable, despite history — risk-benefit in life-threatening situation)
Iodine solution (SSKI or Lugol's) — given at least 1 hour AFTER antithyroid drug to block new hormone synthesis
Propranolol IV for heart rate control (preferred beta-blocker in thyroid storm — also blocks T4 to T3 conversion)
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV q8h (blocks T4 to T3 conversion and addresses possible relative adrenal insufficiency)
Aggressive cooling measures for hyperthermia (cooling blanket, acetaminophen — AVOID aspirin as it displaces thyroid hormone from binding proteins)
IV fluids for dehydration from diarrhea, fever, and hypermetabolic state
Glucose monitoring (hypermetabolic state can cause hypoglycemia)
ICU admission — thyroid storm has 10-30% mortality
Social work to arrange care for 6-year-old daughter
Medication assistance program referral to prevent future non-compliance
Endocrinology consult for definitive treatment planning (radioactive iodine vs. thyroidectomy given recurrence)
